options.B: Only inputs, with no outputs provided
options.C: Only outputs, with no inputs provided
options.D: Randomly generated data with no structure

options.B: A vector orthogonal to both inputs

questions.explanation: L1 regularization adds the sum of absolute weights to the loss, encouraging sparsity and reducing overfitting.

options.A: L1 penalty (sum of absolute values of weights)
options.B: A constant offset added to all predictions
options.C: A penalty on the number of training examples
options.D: A penalty that increases with higher learning rates

questions.question: You are training a logistic regression model and want to reduce overfitting by shrinking coefficients toward zero. Which regularization term should you add to the loss?

questions.question: In PyTorch, which call is typically required to clear accumulated gradients before the next backward pass during training?

questions.explanation: Subword methods handle rare/unknown words by decomposing them into frequent pieces, improving coverage and efficiency.

options.A: It eliminates the need for any vocabulary
options.B: It guarantees perfect grammar in generated text
options.C: It reduces out-of-vocabulary issues by representing rare words as smaller units
options.D: It makes training unnecessary because tokens are pre-labeled

questions.question: In NLP, why is subword tokenization (e.g., BPE/WordPiece) commonly used instead of word-level tokenization?

questions.explanation: To attribute effects to the architecture, you must control confounders by holding other factors constant and changing only the architecture.

options.A: Change the architecture and also change the dataset and preprocessing to match the new model
options.B: Use the same dataset, preprocessing, training budget, and evaluation protocol; change only the architecture
options.C: Evaluate the new model on a different test set that is easier to compare visually
options.D: Tune the new model extensively but do not tune the baseline to save time

questions.question: You are designing an experiment to test whether a new model architecture improves accuracy over a baseline. Which design choice best supports a causal claim that the architecture change caused the improvement?

questions.explanation: If near-duplicates appear across splits, the model can effectively memorize, inflating validation performance without true generalization.

options.A: Using random horizontal flips during training augmentation
options.B: Normalizing images using mean and standard deviation computed from the training set only
options.C: Splitting the dataset after generating near-duplicate crops/frames so that highly similar images appear in both train and validation
options.D: Using a smaller batch size for validation than for training

questions.question: In computer vision, you suspect data leakage because validation accuracy is extremely high early in training. Which scenario is the most plausible source of leakage?

questions.explanation: Subtracting a baseline (like V(s)) from returns reduces variance of gradient estimates while keeping the expected gradient unchanged.

options.A: Add a baseline value function and use advantage estimates (e.g., A(s,a) = Q(s,a) - V(s))
options.B: Increase the discount factor above 1 to emphasize long-term rewards
options.C: Remove exploration by always taking the greedy action
options.D: Replace rewards with random noise to encourage robustness

questions.question: In reinforcement learning, you want to reduce variance of policy gradient updates without introducing bias. Which technique accomplishes this?
